title: Pilot adoption: partner/navigator uses the guides to help real people (outcome recorded)
context: Benefits-Navigator is an openly-licensed corpus of plain-language, primary-source-cited, multilingual, accessible guides to public-assistance programs (SNAP, Medicaid/CHIP, WIC, TANF, LIHEAP, SSI, housing, EITC, Lifeline/ACP), delivered as a static site, printable PDFs, and structured data with an optional retrieval-grounded explainer. It is a HIGH risk-tier project: it provides information, not legal/benefits advice, never makes an individualized eligibility determination, and always routes people to the official application and free human help. No partner, credentialed reviewer, or requestor is yet secured. This task closes the loop - the deed: the pilot partner/navigator demonstrably uses the guides to help real people understand or access a program, with at least one recorded outcome and the partner's attestation, not-advice framing upheld and figures current throughout. Gated on a secured partner (TO BE SECURED).
objective: Record the closed-loop outcome: the pilot partner/navigator uses the guides to help real people, with >= 1 recorded beneficiary outcome and the partner's attestation.
acceptanceCriteria: The pilot partner/navigator demonstrably uses the guides to help real people understand/access a program; >= 1 outcome is recorded (a person helped, or a navigator's verified time-saved / fewer-wrong-applications result), with the partner's attestation
Not-advice framing + route-to-human upheld throughout; figures current (no stale claims served)
Outcome recorded against the project Definition of Shipped (a real beneficiary outcome, not page views)
